_comment: Copy to node.local.json (gitignored) and fill in your own paths. Placeholders {hub_root}/{pid}/{cache_root}/{sid}/{YYYY-MM} resolve at runtime. The watcher (src/audio_inbox_watch.py) reads this file.
_transcribe_cli: optional: path to media_transcribe_cli.py; defaults to the one next to the watcher
_sources_note: discover=project-inboxes scans {hub_root}/_inbox AND {hub_root}/<pid>/_<pid>_inbox/ AND {hub_root}/<pid>/ top-level files; pid = the <pid> folder name. For a single flat inbox instead use: {"root":"{hub_root}/_inbox","route":"mapper","claim":true}.

outputs._voiceprints_note: project_projection = where the per-project speaker registry (index.json + profiles/) lives; set to {hub_root}/{pid} to keep voiceprints at the project root. local_cache = node-local dir for the embeddings store (voiceprints.json), kept OFF the shared hub. Active only when voiceprint_mode != off (see below).

_voiceprint_mode_note: off = diarization only (SPEAKER_00/01…); enroll = build the project registry from new voices (first bootstrap pass); match = identify speakers against the existing registry. Requires speaker_mode: diarize + HF token.
_ktalk_note: A Ktalk (Kontur.Talk) download is a media file + a transcript naming the speakers. When one is found next to the media, speakers are taken from it and diarization is skipped (ASR still runs). {stem} = media file name without extension; [] disables the lookup. See docs/speaker-recognition.md.
_cleanup_note: keep the hub tidy. copy_asr_chunk_artifacts:false stops decoded chunk WAV/JSON from landing in outputs (set true to keep crash-recovery chunks). cleanup_intermediates:true prunes raw/merged dumps + the asr/ scratch dir after a successful run, keeping transcript + segments + run-meta.
_scan_knobs: which files the watcher picks up; omit any key to use built-in defaults
_cloud_watchdog: abort a wedged CloudStorage syscall — macOS Google Drive (File Provider) can hang a recursive listdir/stat/open on a dataless subtree indefinitely at 0% CPU, with no Errno of its own. scan_op_timeout_seconds bounds each per-source discovery scan (wedged source is logged + skipped); cloud_op_timeout_seconds bounds the per-file pickup. Make a source available-offline to include one that keeps timing out. No-op on Windows (no SIGALRM).
_scheduling_knobs: CPU-aware deferral + retry policy + optional time window
_multi_node: opt-in cross-machine coordination via per-file claim.json
